title: Skopeo intermittent auth token retrieval failure
body:
Hi,

I am having issues getting consistent login behaviour to docker.io.

When executing the following command -
```
skopeo inspect --debug docker://reportportal/service-authorization:5.14.1
```
This is always returned -
```
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf"
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/001-rhel-shortnames.conf"
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/002-rhel-shortnames-overrides.conf"
DEBU[0000] Trying to access "docker.io/reportportal/service-authorization:5.14.1"
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching docker.io/reportportal/service-authorization found in /run/containers/0/auth.json
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching docker.io/reportportal/service-authorization found in /root/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for docker.io/reportportal/service-authorization in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /root/.docker/config.json
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/reportportal/service-authorization:5.14.1, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
DEBU[0001] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0001] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?account=mydockerhubaccount&scope=repository%3Areportportal%2Fservice-authorization%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io
```

Usually this works, authenticates successfully, and returns the manifest -
```
DEBU[0001] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/reportportal/service-authorization/manifests/5.14.1
DEBU[0002] Content-Type from manifest GET is "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json"
...
```

However, sometimes the following happens and the manifest is not returned -
```
DEBU[0001] Accessing "docker.io/reportportal/service-authorization:5.14.1" failed: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: incorrect username or password
FATA[0001] Error parsing image name "docker://reportportal/service-authorization:5.14.1": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: incorrect username or password
```

Occurrence of the error appears totally random.  Also, the issue is not limited to `skopeo inspect`.

My environment is as follows -
I am behind a proxy with the below installed on my device.
```
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
VERSION="9.6 (Plow)"

skopeo version 1.18.1

docker version
Client: Docker Engine - Community
 Version:           28.1.1
 API version:       1.49
 Go version:        go1.23.8
 Git commit:        4eba377
 Built:             Fri Apr 18 09:54:03 2025
 OS/Arch:           linux/amd64
 Context:           default

Server: Docker Engine - Community
 Engine:
  Version:          28.1.1
  API version:      1.49 (minimum version 1.24)
  Go version:       go1.23.8
  Git commit:       01f442b
  Built:            Fri Apr 18 09:52:20 2025
  OS/Arch:          linux/amd64
  Experimental:     false
 containerd:
  Version:          1.7.27
  GitCommit:        05044ec0a9a75232cad458027ca83437aae3f4da
 runc:
  Version:          1.2.5
  GitCommit:        v1.2.5-0-g59923ef
 docker-init:
  Version:          0.19.0
  GitCommit:        de40ad0

```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

I think the initial hypothesis must be that this is caused server-side; given a specific environment, Skopeo should be deterministic about the credentials it sends — while intermittent failures of overloaded servers are somewhat expected.

Server-side logs _might_ provide more information about the authorization failure; the server is certainly not going to send details to a possible attacker.

In the worst case, there’s always `--retry-times`, although that can only help so much.

Thanks for the reply.

It could be on the docker.io side.  However, `pcap` scans show clear traffic all the way with no denial of service response.

Also, I don't get any failures if I `curl` the same URL
```
curl -v https://auth.docker.io/token?...
```

`curl` does not fail regardless of how often or how quickly I attempt the connection.

That error is, AFAIK, _only_ reported when the client receives a HTTP status 401. So, there should be _some_ corresponding server-side (or proxy-side?) activity / decision.

I’m afraid Skopeo does not currently have built-in HTTP tracing options; if you are familiar with Go, you can use https://github.com/containers/image/pull/201 .

I can confirm the network I am on prevents icmp traffic.  Hence, I always get a status 401 on the ping, regardless of whether skopeo works or not -
```
DEBU[0000]  Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
```

Another test I have been doing is with docker login, which never fails -
```
docker login
```

However, the equivalent skopeo login fails after every few attempts -
```
skopeo login docker.io
```

> I can confirm the network I am on prevents icmp traffic. Hence, I always get a status 401 on the ping

That’s not how that works: this is a “ping” over a HTTP API, not an ICMP ping. (ICMP ping’s don’t fail with HTTP statuses). And, a registry replying in 401 on this ping is fine and expected.

> However, the equivalent skopeo login fails after every few attempts -
>
> `skopeo login docker.io`

That’s interesting (and it would make sense for a server to rate-limit authentication); also, I don’t think there is _anything_ about that that can be fixed client-side.

For ordinary operation, not logins, https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1968 _might_ make a difference, but that’s an unsubstantiated guess; only the servers’ operators can tell for sure what the failure cause is. Consider reaching out to the server’s support contact.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> I think the initial hypothesis must be that this is caused server-side

Please reopen if there is any data that contradicts this.
